reasoning: The informal statement says: if every infinite subset of a metric space X has a limit point, then X is separable. The Lean signature requires X to be a MetricSpace, hypothesizes that every infinite subset A has some p : X with `AccPt p (𝓟 A)`, and concludes `SeparableSpace X`. In Mathlib, `AccPt p (𝓟 A)` unfolds to `NeBot (𝓝[≠] p ⊓ 𝓟 A)`, meaning every punctured neighborhood of p meets A — exactly Rudin's definition of a limit point of A (the point need not lie in A, matching the informal reading). `SeparableSpace X` is the standard "has a countable dense subset," matching "separable." Quantifier structure and types all align, so this is a faithful formalization.
